---
title: 'Species Name: Residual conductance'
author: 'Your Name'
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output:
  prettydoc::html_pretty:
    highlight: pygments
    theme: cayman
    toc: yes
    number_sections: no
    toc_depth: 1
---

```{r, setup_wd, include=FALSE}
# Set knit directory
setwd(here::here(EDITME))
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = setwd(here::here(EDITME)))
getwd()
```

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
options(scipen = 999)

# Save figures in specific place
knitr::opts_chunk$set(autodep        = TRUE,
                      cache          = FALSE,
                      cache.comments = TRUE,
                      comment = "",
                      # Save figures as pdf ?
                      #dev = c( "png", "pdf"),
                  
                      # Include code?
                      echo           = TRUE,
                      error          = FALSE,
                      fig.align      = "center",
                      
                      # Path where figures are going to be store pdf single 
                      # figures
                      Edit path in line 44 and comment out this line   
                      fig.path       = paste0("./figures_[sp_name]", "/"),
                      fig.width      = 15,
                      fig.height     = 13,
                      message        = FALSE,
                      warning        = FALSE)
```


```{r cleanup-docs, cache = FALSE,echo = FALSE}
# save a html copy file in a specific place
# doc.files <- c(list.files(pattern = "pdf"),
#                list.files(pattern = "html"),
#                list.files(pattern = "docx"))
# 
# for (file in doc.files) {   
#     file.rename(file, file.path("../../[insert_folder_name]/", file))
# }
```

```{r libaries, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, cache = FALSE}
library(HIEdroughtbox)
library(ggplot2)

# For data manipulation
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(here)

# For adding regression line to ggplot
library(ggpubr)
```

```{r echo=FALSE}
EDITME
tree_id_colors <- c("1#1" = "#eec000",
                   "1#2" = "#cf544c",
                   "1#3" = "#0175c3",
                   "1#4" = "#878687",
                   
                   "1#5" =  "#000000",
                   "1#6" =  "#008b28",
                   "1#7" =  "#8600b6",
                   "1#8" =  "#00a0ab")

string_colors <- c(string_1 = "#eec000",
                   string_2 = "#cf544c",
                   string_3 = "#0175c3",
                   string_4 = "#878687",
                   
                   string_5 =  "#000000",
                   string_6 =  "#008b28",
                   string_7 =  "#8600b6",
                   string_8 =  "#00a0ab")


# Function for adding the number of observations on top of a boxplot
give_n <- function(x){
  return(c(y = median(x)*2, label = length(x))) 
  # experiment with the multiplier to find the perfect position
}
```

# 1. Load data

## Load water loss data

```{r, load_water_loss_data, eval = FALSE}
load("./[sp_name]_processed_data/[sp_name]_water_loss_rates.RData")
```

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# Show the names of each element in the list
glimpse([sp_name]_water_loss_data)
```

## Load cleaned droughtbox data

```{r, load_droughtbox_data_with_rwc, eval = FALSE}
load("./[sp_name]_processed_data/[sp_name]_rwc_filtered_data.RData")

[sp_name]_data_for_gres <- [sp_name]_data_for_water_loss
```

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# Show the names of each element in the list
glimpse([sp_name]_data_for_gres)
```

# 3. Calculate species residual conductance 

## Calculate Residual conductance

```{r, get_vpd_values, eval = FALSE}
# Atmospheric pressure in the droughtbox constant -------------------------------
atmospheric_pressure_constant = 101.6

# Get VPD data ------------------------------------------------------------------

# [sp_name]_vpd_data <-
        [sp_name]_data_for_gres %>%
        dplyr::select(time_seconds,
                      string_number,
                      leaf_dry_weight_g,
                      set_temperature,
                      vpd_control,
                      vpd_avg_kpa_avg)
```


```{r, get_water_loss_rates, eval = FALSE}
# Filter transpiration rates ----------------------------------------------------

# [sp_name]_water_loss_data <-
  
        [sp_name]_water_loss_data %>% 
        dplyr::select(spcode,
                      tree_id,
                      string_number,
                      set_temperature,
                      vpd_control,
                      leaf_areas_m2, 
                      transpiration_double_grams_per_sec_m2)

```

```{r, calculate_gres, eval = FALSE}
# Estimate residual conductance -------------------------------------------------

# [sp_name]_residual_conductance_df <-

        # Join data
        dplyr::full_join([sp_name]_water_loss_data, 
                         [sp_name]_vpd_data,
                         by = join_by(string_number, 
                                      set_temperature, 
                                      vpd_control)) %>% 

        # Sort columns
        dplyr::select(spcode, time_seconds, everything()) %>%

        # Calculate variables
        dplyr::mutate(

            # Transform grams of water to mols of water
            transpiration_mol_per_sec_m2 = transpiration_double_grams_per_sec_m2/18.015,


            # Calculate gres as (E/VPD)*101.6
            gres = (transpiration_mol_per_sec_m2/vpd_avg_kpa_avg)*101.6,

            # Transform traspiration to Milimols of water
            transpiration_mmol_per_sec_m2 = (transpiration_double_grams_per_sec_m2/18.015)*1000) %>%

        dplyr::group_by(tree_id, vpd_control, set_temperature, 
                        leaf_areas_m2, leaf_dry_weight_g) 

        # Get the median values
        dplyr::summarise(gres_mols_m2_s = median(gres),
                         transpiration_H2Ommol_per_sec_m2 = median(transpiration_mol_per_sec_m2))

```


```{r, echo = FALSE, eval = FALSE}
[sp_name]_residual_conductance_df %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 3)) %>%
    reactable::reactable(.)
```

### Plot Residual conductance data

```{r, plot_residual_conductance, fig.width = 16, fig.height = 8, eval = FALSE, echo = FALSE}

ggplot(data = [sp_name]_residual_conductance_df, aes(x = set_temperature,
                                                     y = gres_mols_m2_s,
                                                  
                             colour = factor(tree_id))) +
        
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  
        geom_point(size = 3.5) +
  
        # Increase the tickmarks of the axes
        scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 12)) + 
        scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 7)) +
  
        # Position label at the bottom
        theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
    
        facet_wrap(~vpd_control) +
        
        # Set colors
        #scale_color_manual(values = c("#9e1523","#293352")) +
        ylab('Residual conductance (mols*m-2*s-1)')+
        xlab("Temperature") +
        scale_color_manual(values = tree_id_colors) 
```

### Plot transpiration data

```{r, plot_transpiration,fig.width = 16, fig.height = 8, eval = FALSE, echo = FALSE}

ggplot(data = [sp_name]_residual_conductance_df, aes(x = set_temperature,
                                                     y = transpiration_H2Ommol_per_sec_m2,
                                                     colour = factor(tree_id))) +
        
        theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  
        geom_point(size = 3.5) +
  
        # Increase the tickmarks of the axes
        scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 12)) + 
        scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 7)) +
  
        # Position label at the bottom
        theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
    
        facet_wrap(~vpd_control) +
        
        # Set colors
        #scale_color_manual(values = c("#9e1523","#293352")) +
        ylab('Transpiration (mmols*m-2*s-1)')+
        xlab("Temperature") +
        scale_color_manual(values = tree_id_colors) 
```

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# Add spcode to data before saving 

#[sp_name]_residual_conductance_data <- 
  
  [sp_name]_residual_conductance_df %>% 
    
  mutate(spcode = "[sp_name]") %>% 
  select(spcode, everything())
```

# 5. Export data

```{r, export_data, eval = FALSE}
save([sp_name]_residual_conductance_data, 
     file = "./[sp_name]_processed_data/[sp_name]_residual_conductance_data.RData")
```
